question: [File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getTopicListener()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the UnsupportedOperationException thrown by getTopicListener() for unknown listener types to probe the server's configuration and infer which listener backend is active, then tailor their unauthorized subscription attack to the specific backend's weaknesses?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that the TopicMessageFilter builder pattern (using @Builder) allows constructing a filter with any topicId without any ownership assertion, and then pass this filter to listen() to receive all messages from a topic they are not authorized to access?
